Container.Name: iDRAC-Fan-Controller-Rust
Container.Overview:
Dell iDRAC Fan Controller with Exponential Curve - Rust Edition
This controller provides smooth, progressive fan speed control using an exponential curve instead of binary on/off switching.
Key Features: - Exponential fan curve for smooth transitions - Configurable temperature thresholds and curve steepness - Hysteresis to prevent oscillation - Auto-restore Dell default fan control on exit - Low resource usage (~0.1% CPU, ~5MB RAM)
IMPORTANT: Read SAFE_TESTING.md before first use!
Compatible with Dell PowerEdge servers with iDRAC firmware < 3.30.30.30

Config.Name: iDRAC Host
Config.Description: iDRAC IP address or 'local' for local IPMI device

Config.Name: iDRAC Username
Config.Description: iDRAC username (not needed for local mode)

Config.Name: iDRAC Password
Config.Description: iDRAC password (not needed for local mode)

Config.Name: Base Temperature
Config.Description: Temperature (°C) where minimum fan speed is applied

Config.Name: Critical Temperature
Config.Description: Temperature (°C) where maximum fan speed is applied

Config.Name: Curve Steepness
Config.Description: Exponential curve steepness (higher = more aggressive, typical range 0.1-0.3)

Config.Name: Disable Third-Party PCIe Cooling
Config.Description: Disable Dell default cooling response for third-party PCIe cards (Gen 13 and older only)

Config.Name: Keep PCIe State on Exit
Config.Description: Keep third-party PCIe cooling state on exit instead of restoring Dell default

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Log level: debug, info, warn, error

Config.Name: IPMI Device
Config.Description: Local IPMI device (only needed for local mode, remove if using remote iDRAC)
